title: Recurrent-event stop model with stop-number strata
summary: Pit stops are recurrent events, and recurrent-event literature recommends modeling event history explicitly rather than only time-to-first-event.
rationale: Pit stops are recurrent events, and recurrent-event literature recommends modeling event history explicitly rather than only time-to-first-event. In F1 terms, first-stop, second-stop, and late splash-and-dash behavior have different hazards, so a single pooled binary target can blur signal.
implementation_hint: Create leakage-safe features for prior stop count, laps since last stop, stint index, and interactions with compound/weather, then train separate heads or a shared model with stop-number strata for stop 0->1, 1->2, and 2+; blend their probabilities back to one PitNextLap score based on current prior-stop count.
expected_effect: Should sharpen calibration and ranking because the model no longer forces one hazard shape across fundamentally different strategy phases.
risk: Small sample sizes for high stop counts can overfit; if validation is not grouped by race, recurrent-history features may look better than they generalize.
